privacyPolicy.inAppLocation: Settings > External Display HUD > App Preferences > Privacy Policy

appAccess.reviewAccessInstructions: Open the app, go to Settings > External Display HUD > App Preferences, and enable Demo Mode. It exposes the HUD without an account, credentials, pairing code, purchase, or external hardware; gateway pairing is optional. Reviewers can inspect chat, notifications, voice/caption controls, display placement, and external-display settings. Each assistant response has a flag button that opens the offensive-content report form and displays the exact submitted-data disclosure before confirmation.

targetAudience.rationale: OpenClaw HUD is a developer/operator assistant HUD for Android external-display workflows, not a children-directed app.

contentRating.notes: No gambling, financial trading, dating, UGC social feed, or ads. Assistant responses depend on the user-configured OpenClaw gateway and selected model/provider.

aiGeneratedContent.notes: Assistant response bubbles expose an in-app flag action. The confirmation dialog discloses the bounded payload and submits it directly to the ECO Systems LLC receiver without leaving the app. Live endpoint health and receipt-backed POST storage were verified on 2026-07-15.

dataDeletion.userDeletionPath: Android Settings > Apps > OpenClaw HUD > Storage > Clear data, or uninstall the app.
dataDeletion.notes: The HUD app does not create an OpenClaw cloud account. Gateway accounts, logs, and model-provider data are controlled by the user's configured gateway/provider.

dataSafety.dataEncryptedInTransitNotes: Public gateway URLs require wss:// or Tailscale Serve. The app also permits cleartext ws:// only for localhost, Android emulator, private LAN, link-local, and Tailnet-style addresses so developers can connect to self-hosted gateways. Do not answer that all collected user data is encrypted in transit unless these self-hosted cleartext paths are disabled before submission.

collectedData.whereUsed: Wake word, push-to-talk, and realtime caption/translation workflows when enabled by the user.
collectedData.destination: Configured OpenClaw gateway and the model/provider selected through that gateway.

collectedData.whereUsed: HUD chat prompts, assistant responses, and notification summaries when the user connects to a gateway. A user-confirmed offensive-content report sends only the selected assistant excerpt, category, optional note, app version, and one-way message hash.
collectedData.destination: Configured OpenClaw gateway; for an explicit offensive-content report only, the ECO Systems LLC receiver at aops.studio.

collectedData.whereUsed: Assistant run controls plus active session, agent, provider, and model selections when enabled.
Gateway connection state, external-display capability status, optional AirVision profile state, and diagnostics exports created explicitly by the user.
collectedData.destination: Stored locally unless the user explicitly sends or exports diagnostics.

reviewEvidence.demoMode: Settings > External Display HUD > App Preferences > Demo Mode

Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows Spatial & Mirror Controls > Cast
Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows Spatial & Mirror Controls > Display
Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Link per-control WAIT/CAPTURE/READY rows

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: HUD presentation and DeX display targeting
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Enable Demo Mode, then open the HUD tab or launch the HUD presentation.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Fallback screenshots and CI exercise the phone review path; live M1 testing is only needed before claiming display-specific framing on hardware.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 profile controls, profile backup/import, and Windows App Handoff export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Saved Android values, per-profile runtime summaries, and export text are generated without raw USB serials.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 profile brightness, HUD dimming preview, and M1 touch-bar hardware brightness.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Software HUD dimming is reviewable offline; live M1 testing proves panel brightness behavior and firmware touch-bar passthrough.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 profile distance and HUD scale controls.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Virtual HUD distance scaling, per-profile HUD scale, and runtime summaries are generated without a live M1.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: IPD, fit, clarity, and text size
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 IPD, Fit & Clarity guidance, and diagnostics export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: IPD calibration defaults to 67 mm and fit guidance is reviewable offline; physical focus proof needs the live M1.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Splendid, Eye Care, and blue-light filter
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 Splendid and Eye Care profile controls.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Android previews HUD color/warmth states offline; true panel preset writes need ASUS HID protocol evidence.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Motion Sync, 3D Mode, and Light Load Mode
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 Motion Sync, 3D Mode, Light Load Mode, and runtime diagnostics.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Light Load trims Android HUD work offline; Motion Sync and panel 3D writes remain firmware-protocol gated.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Gesture & Hotkey Settings and Diagnostics Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Single-tap, double-tap, swipe, brightness-key, and media-key mappings are stored offline. Settings and diagnostics export shortcut-menu parity for ASUS brightness/volume/distance behavior, plus the active M1 brightness-key/media-key mode, Android consumption state, step sizes, firmware passthrough expectation, and Windows gesture catalog entries for ASUS brightness swipe, play/pause tap, instant transparent, center virtual screens, 3D hold, and shortcut-menu hold/slide behavior; live M1 proof is needed for firmware-delivered events.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Link and Diagnostics Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Firmware Link shows per-control WAIT/CAPTURE/READY rows with target value, Android effect, firmware status, missing evidence, and blocker text. Offline demo shows blocked/write-readiness states; connected M1 adds live descriptor and HID report-path context.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Updates > Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Android write actions remain blocked until sanitized ASUS HID capture evidence and live M1 write tests are both present.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Samsung/native captions and OpenClaw translation fallback
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Voice tab caption controls and Samsung/native caption launcher.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Native captioning is delegated to Android/Samsung UI; OpenClaw fallback exports structured caption-mode status with sage-router fast profile, thinking off, selected source/target languages, and S1/S2 speaker labels.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > App Preferences and Profile Backup.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Startup view, display target, language, speaker, visible structured native captions plus OpenClaw translation fallback status, translation languages, demo mode, structured support/legal/registration metadata, privacy/support links, and JSON backup/import are reviewable offline.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows App Handoff > Export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: The Android app exports Cyber/Windows ASUS AirVision apply steps, runtime summaries, gesture mappings, row-level observed Windows surfaces/keys/defaults/capture implications, structured caption/translation mode status, structured support/legal/registration metadata, and privacy reminders without raw USB serials.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Firmware Link and diagnostics export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: USB manufacturer/product, vendor/product ID, descriptor version, and interface details populate when the M1 is connected.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Identify or Demo Mode HUD presentation.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Android can show a temporary HUD 1 marker on the Presentation display for reviewer and external-display identification.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Display Target, presentation routing diagnostics, and DeX launch script.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: External-display targeting and physical main-screen visibility are configurable offline; final DeX/M1 topology proof needs a live external display.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Windows-only spatial controls and Windows App Handoff export.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: Cursor Follow, Center Cursor, and 3DoF are reported as Windows-only with structured fallback state. Android provides distance-hotkey mapping status, hardware touchpad passthrough status, and limitation text explaining the required ASUS Windows app surfaces.

airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.capability: Unity mirror window / projected glasses view
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.reviewPath: Settings > AirVision M1 > Cast, Display, and Windows App Handoff mirror fallback guidance.
airVisionCompanionCapabilityStates.evidence: The ASUS Unity mirror window and Ctrl+Alt+E shortcut stay Windows-only; Settings, diagnostics, and Windows App Handoff provide structured Cast, Display, and Samsung DeX sharing fallback actions plus limitations.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.windowsAppTarget: Working, Gaming, Infinity, Custom 1, or Custom 2
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Per-mode Android profile slot and custom label state
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Reviewable offline in Settings, profile backup, Windows App Handoff, and diagnostics export.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Software HUD dimming plus M1 hardware touch-bar passthrough
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Software dimming is reviewable offline; panel brightness proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: HUD scale derived from virtual distance and per-profile HUD scale
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: HUD scaling is reviewable offline; firmware-level distance write proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Stored calibration, ASUS range check, and fit/clarity guidance
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Calibration and guidance are reviewable offline; physical focus proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.windowsAppTarget: Standard, Theater, Office, Game, Eye Care, and blue-light percentage
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: HUD color and warmth preview overlays
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Preview overlays are reviewable offline; true panel preset proof needs live M1 hardware.
Stored setting is reviewable offline; hardware behavior proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Low-overhead HUD mode with trimmed transcript/caption history and locked IPD/3D controls
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Android runtime behavior is reviewable offline; panel state proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Stored desired state, locked off when Light Load Mode is enabled
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Android guard state is reviewable offline; panel 3D proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: HUD scale, placement, safe area, and physical main-screen visibility
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Reviewable offline with Demo Mode and screenshots; live display proof is optional for M1 framing.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Android Presentation display targeting for AirVision-preferred or other external displays
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Routing settings are reviewable offline; DeX/M1 topology proof needs live external display hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Single tap, double tap, swipe, brightness-key, and media-key mappings
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Stored mappings are reviewable offline; firmware-delivered event proof needs live M1 hardware.

airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.windowsAppTarget: Cursor Follow, Center Cursor, 3DoF, or Unity mirror when needed
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.androidEffect: Windows-only status plus Android Cast, Display, and DeX fallback guidance
airVisionWindowsApplyMatrixReview.proof: Android fallback and Windows-only status are reviewable offline; ASUS spatial/mirror features remain Windows workflows.

airVisionWindowsAppEvidenceReview.proof: Windows App Handoff and diagnostics export include Cyber-observed ASUS AirVision version/build/settings-key evidence for reviewer inspection without a live M1.
airVisionWindowsAppEvidenceReview.privacyBoundary: The exported evidence omits raw HID bytes, raw USB serial values, user-specific paths, gateway endpoints, auth tokens, and chat history.

reviewEvidence.airVisionCompanionReviewNotes: Demo Mode lets reviewers verify the 18-feature AirVision companion HUD catalog, 12-row Windows app apply matrix, structured M1 hardware-key diagnostics, and Cyber-observed ASUS AirVision 1.0.7.1 settings-key evidence without a live gateway or live M1. Cast and Display open Android or DeX mirror fallback settings outside the HUD. Firmware-update handoff, Windows app handoff, and diagnostics exports are user-initiated files that omit raw USB serial values; Android firmware writes remain blocked until validated ASUS HID protocol evidence exists.

appCreatedInPlayConsole.source: Google Play Console > All apps > OpenClaw HUD (ai.openclaw.app.hud)
appCreatedInPlayConsole.notes: Verified that the separate OpenClaw HUD app exists as Play app 4973554177385209084.

internalTestersConfiguredInPlayConsole.source: Google Play Console > Test and release > Testing > Internal testing > Testers
internalTestersConfiguredInPlayConsole.notes: Verified that the dedicated OpenClaw HUD Internal Testers list is attached with one intended tester.

reviewerAccessConfiguredInPlayConsole.source: Google Play Console > Policy and programs > App content > Sign in details
reviewerAccessConfiguredInPlayConsole.notes: Verified that Play contains account-free Demo Mode instructions covering the HUD without a gateway or external hardware, plus the assistant-response flag and report flow.

finalSubmission.notes: Play app creation, internal tester selection, privacy policy, reviewer Demo Mode and reporting access, store listing, category/contact details, and signed version 2026071601 upload were verified through 2026-07-16. The internal release contains only version 2026071601, adds frame shapes, broader external input routing, and unlock recovery, and remains a draft. Data safety includes Developer communications for optional Other in-app messages. These changes have not been sent for review or rolled out.
